addBot.title: Add the bot to a server
addBot.summary: Invite Gandhi to your Discord server in a few clicks.
addBot.intro: Gandhi tracks voice activity and turns it into stats. Here's how to bring it to your server.
addBot.step1: You need the “Manage Server” permission on the Discord server.
addBot.step2: Click “Add to your server” below — it opens Discord's authorization page.
addBot.step3: Pick your server from the list and confirm the permissions.
addBot.step4: Back in Discord, opt in to start tracking. Learn how to manage what's tracked:
addBot.disclaimer: Gandhi only requests the permissions it needs to read voice states and post logs — nothing more. It never reads your messages, and members are only tracked once they opt in.
addBot.permsNote: It only requests the permissions it needs to read voice states and post logs.

commands.title: Commands
commands.summary: The slash commands you can use in Discord.
commands.intro: Type these in any channel where the bot is present.
commands.heatmap: Render your activity as a heatmap image.
commands.userSettings: Manage your own tracking preferences (opt-in, private mode).
commands.serverSettings: Server admins: configure tracking, logs and the log channel.
commands.export: Download everything the bot holds about you.
commands.deleteData: Erase all your data on a server (or everywhere).
commands.resetStats: Reset your stats to zero, keeping your settings.
commands.myServers: List the servers where you have data.

args.targetAll: Aggregate every member of the server.
args.stat: Which stat to use (default: time connected).
args.togglePrivate: Turn private mode on or off.
args.logchannel: The text channel used for logs.

prefs.title: Preferences & consent
prefs.summary: What the bot tracks, and how you stay in control.
prefs.intro: The bot only tracks you once you opt in — and you can change everything at any time.

stats.title: Stats tracking (opt-in)
stats.body: Off by default. When on, the bot records your voice durations to build your stats.

logs.title: Event logging (opt-in)
logs.body: When on, the bot posts your voice events (join, mute, …) in the server's log channel.

private.title: Private mode
private.body: Hides you from leaderboards and other members' views, while still counting in anonymous server totals.

consent.title: Your consent
consent.body: Both the server and your personal settings must be enabled for any tracking to happen — so you're never tracked without agreeing.

prefs.manage: Manage all of this from your

data.title: Your personal data
data.summary: Access, export and erase the data the bot holds.
data.intro: You are always in control of your data.

access.title: Access & export
access.body: Download all your data as JSON or CSV from your profile, or with /export in Discord.

erase.title: Erasure
erase.body: Permanently erase your data on a server, or everywhere, from your profile or with /delete-data.

reset.title: Reset
reset.body: Zero your stats while keeping your settings and history, from your profile or with /reset-stats.

what.title: What is stored
what.body: Only your Discord user id and aggregate voice durations per server — never message content.

profile.subtitle: Your language and tracking preferences.
profile.languageHint: Choose the language of the dashboard.
profile.prefsHint: Control what the bot tracks and shows about you.
profile.matrixHint: Use the “All servers” row to change a setting everywhere, or toggle it per server.
profile.noServers: You don't share any server with the bot yet.
profile.supportHint: Guides on adding the bot, commands, preferences and your data.

data.title: Your data
data.hint: Export, reset or permanently erase the data the bot holds about you.

export.title: Export your data
export.desc: Download everything we hold about you (stats, daily history, settings).

reset.title: Reset your stats
reset.desc: Set your aggregate stats back to zero. Your settings and daily history are kept.
reset.confirm: Your stats on {{scope}} will be reset to zero. The totals cannot be recovered. Continue?

delete.title: Delete your data
delete.desc: Permanently erase everything on the selected scope — stats, daily history and settings. You revert to the default (not tracked).
delete.confirm: This will permanently delete all data we hold about you on {{scope}}. This cannot be undone.
delete.logsNote: Log messages already posted in server channels are not affected — ask a server admin to remove them if needed.
delete.done: Your data was permanently deleted ({{count}} server(s)).

meta.title: Gandhi Bot — Voice activity stats for Discord

hero.title: Turn Discord voice time into
hero.subtitle: Gandhi tracks how your community hangs out in voice channels — time connected, streaks, screen shares and more — and turns it into a live dashboard, a yearly heatmap and server leaderboards.
hero.reassurance: Free · no message content is ever read · members must opt in.

features.title: Everything your voice channels get up to
features.subtitle: From a single join to a year of activity — Gandhi measures it, and only when everyone agreed to it.

voice.title: Voice activity tracking
voice.desc: Time connected, muted, deafened, screen sharing and camera — captured live from voice-state changes.

heatmap.title: Yearly heatmap
heatmap.desc: A GitHub-style contribution calendar of your activity, per user or across a whole server.

leaderboard.title: Server leaderboards
leaderboard.desc: Rank members by time, streaks or sessions — with live updates as people join and leave.

privacy.title: Privacy by design
privacy.desc: Double opt-in, a private mode, and one-command export or deletion of everything about you.

demo.title: A year of voice activity, at a glance
demo.subtitle: Every day a member spends in voice lights up the calendar. Hover any cell in the real dashboard to see the exact totals.
demo.lbSubtitle: Rank members by time connected, streaks or sessions — the standings update live as people hop in and out of voice.
demo.lbP1: Ranked by time, streak or sessions
demo.lbP3: Private mode keeps you off the board

commands.title: Everything, right from Discord
commands.subtitle: No dashboard required — manage your stats and your data with a slash command.
commands.heatmap: Render your activity as a heatmap image.
commands.userSettings: Manage your tracking preferences (opt-in, private mode).
commands.export: Download everything the bot holds about you (JSON/CSV).
commands.deleteData: Permanently erase all your data on a server or everywhere.

privacy.title: Nothing is tracked unless you say so
privacy.subtitle: Gandhi is built opt-in from the ground up. A server has to enable tracking, and every member has to opt in for themselves — otherwise nothing about them is recorded.

optIn.title: Double opt-in
optIn.desc: Both the server and the member must enable a feature before anything is stored.

noContent.title: No message content
noContent.desc: Only voice durations and event timestamps — never what you say or type, never any audio.

control.title: Full control
control.desc: Export, reset or delete your data at any time, with a single slash command.

cta.title: Ready to bring Gandhi to your server?
cta.subtitle: Add the bot in a couple of clicks. Members stay in control, and you get the stats.

footer.tagline: Privacy-first voice activity stats for Discord communities.

s1.title: Who is responsible for your data
s1.p1: This policy explains what the Gandhi Bot ("the bot", "we") collects, why, and the rights you have over it. The data controller is:

s2.title: What data we collect
s2.p1: The bot stores the minimum needed to produce statistics. When tracking is active for you, we collect:
items: Your Discord user ID, and the username/avatar exposed by Discord when you sign in to the dashboard.
The IDs of the servers (guilds) you share with the bot.
Voice-state events — joining or leaving a channel, mute, deafen, screen share and camera — with their timestamps and durations.
Aggregate counters and time totals computed from those events, plus a per-day activity history and your personal settings.
s2.note: The bot does not read your messages and never records or stores any audio.

s3.title: Why we process your data
items: To compute and display your voice activity statistics, heatmaps and server leaderboards.
To let you and your server admins manage tracking and logging preferences.
To keep the service running securely and diagnose technical problems.

s4.title: Legal bases (Art. 6 GDPR)
s4.p1: We rely on the following legal bases:

rows.detail: Double opt-in: the server enables the feature and you opt in via /usersettings. You can withdraw at any time.
Needed to sign you in with Discord and show your own data.
Minimal technical logs to keep the service reliable and secure.

s5.title: Where your data is stored
s5.p1: Data is stored in a SQLite database on servers operated by our hosting provider inside the European Union.

s6.title: How long we keep it
s6.p1: Your data lives only as long as it is relevant:
items: It is kept while you remain a member of a server and tracking is active.
Opting out via /usersettings stops any further collection immediately.
Leaving (or being removed from) a server automatically deletes all of your data linked to that server.
You can reset your stats (/reset-stats) or permanently erase everything (/delete-data) at any time.
Backups made by the operator are the only place historical copies may briefly persist before rotating out.

s7.title: Who can access your data
s7.p1: We do not sell your data. It is only shared with the providers strictly needed to run the service:

rows.name: Hetzner Online GmbH
rows.role: Hosting of the database and application

rows.name: Discord Inc.
rows.role: Authentication (OAuth) and the platform the bot runs on

s7.note: Signing in and using Discord is also subject to Discord's own privacy policy.

s8.title: Your rights
s8.p1: Under the GDPR you can exercise the following rights — most of them directly from Discord, with no admin needed:

rights.title: Access
rights.detail: See every server where the bot holds data about you and its tracking status.

rights.title: Portability
rights.detail: Download a copy of all your data in a portable format.

rights.title: Erasure
rights.detail: Permanently delete all data linked to you.

rights.title: Rectification / reset
rights.detail: Reset your stats to zero while keeping your settings.

rights.title: Withdraw consent
rights.detail: Turn off tracking so nothing more is collected.

rights.title: Objection / restriction
rights.detail: Ask us to stop or limit a specific processing.

s8.contact: For any request or question about your data, contact us at

s9.title: Security
s9.p1: We apply reasonable technical and organisational measures to protect your data: restricted access, a hardened server, and collection limited to what is strictly necessary.
s9.p2: Should a data breach likely to affect your rights occur, we will notify the relevant supervisory authority and, where required, the affected users.

s10.title: Cookies
s10.p1: The dashboard uses only strictly necessary cookies. See our

s11.title: Changes to this policy
s11.p1: We may update this policy to reflect changes to the service or the law. The "last updated" date above always reflects the current version; significant changes will be highlighted where possible.

s12.title: Contact and complaints
s12.p1: You can reach the data controller, or lodge a complaint with the French supervisory authority (CNIL):
s12.cnilText: You have the right to lodge a complaint at

s1.title: Purpose of the service
s1.p1: These terms govern your use of the Gandhi Bot and its web dashboard, a service that measures voice activity on Discord servers and turns it into statistics.
s1.note: Gandhi is a free, hobby project provided "as is", with no guaranteed availability or service level.

s2.title: Acceptance
s2.p1: By adding the bot to a server, or by signing in to the dashboard, you accept these terms. If you do not agree, do not use the service.

s3.title: Eligibility
s3.p1: To use the service you must:
items: Comply with Discord's Terms of Service and Community Guidelines.
Meet Discord's minimum age requirement for your country.
Have the necessary permissions on a server before adding the bot to it.

s4.title: Your responsibilities
s4.p1: When using the service you agree to:
items: Use it lawfully and not attempt to disrupt, overload or abuse it.
Not use it to harass, surveil or profile members against their will.
Take responsibility for how you configure and use the data it produces.

s5.title: Server administrators
s5.p1: If you enable tracking or logging on a server you administer, you are responsible for informing its members that their voice activity may be recorded, and for having a lawful basis to do so.
s5.p2: Members always keep their own opt-in switch: nothing is recorded about a member who has not opted in, even where the server has enabled a feature.

s6.title: Intellectual property
s6.p1: The bot's source code is open source and made available under the licence stated in its repository:

s7.title: Limitation of liability
s7.p1: The service is provided without warranty of any kind. To the extent permitted by law, we cannot be held liable for any loss of data, interruption of service, or indirect damage arising from its use.
s7.p2: You remain responsible for your own use of the service and for compliance with the rules that apply to your community.

s8.title: Suspension and termination
s8.p1: We may suspend or discontinue the service, in whole or in part, at any time — for example for maintenance, abuse, or because the project ends. You may stop using it at any time by removing the bot and deleting your data.

s9.title: Changes to these terms
s9.p1: We may update these terms. The "last updated" date reflects the current version, and continued use after a change constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.

s10.title: Governing law
s10.p1: These terms are governed by French law. Any dispute that cannot be resolved amicably will be submitted to the competent French courts.

s11.title: Contact
s11.p1: For any question about these terms, contact us at

notice.title: Legal Notice
notice.authoritative: This notice is provided in accordance with French law (loi n° 2004-575 du 21 juin 2004, LCEN). The French version prevails; this English text is a courtesy translation.

s2.title: Hosting
s2.p1: The website and service are hosted by:

s3.title: Intellectual property
s3.p1: The bot's source code is open source under the licence stated in its repository:

s4.title: Liability
s4.p1: The publisher strives to keep the information provided accurate, but cannot be held responsible for omissions, inaccuracies, or any unavailability of the service.

s5.title: External links
s5.p1: The site links to third-party services (notably Discord and GitHub). The publisher has no control over those sites and accepts no responsibility for their content.

s1.title: What is a cookie?
s1.p1: A cookie is a small file stored by your browser. Similar local storage can also be used to remember your preferences. This page lists everything the dashboard uses.

s2.title: What we use
s2.p1: The dashboard uses only strictly necessary items, required for it to work:

rows.name: gandhi_sid
rows.purpose: Keeps you signed in to the dashboard.

s3.title: No tracking, no third-party cookies
s3.p1: We use no analytics, advertising or third-party cookies. Because all of the above are strictly necessary, no consent banner is required under the ePrivacy rules and CNIL guidance.

s4.title: Your choices and rights
s4.p1: You can delete these items at any time from your browser settings, and sign out to clear the session cookie. Blocking the necessary cookies will prevent the dashboard from working.
s4.p2: You may also lodge a complaint with the CNIL at
s4.contact: For any question, contact us at
